questions.question: The factors of x3 -1 + y3 + 3xy are
questions.explanation:
The given expression to be factorized is x3 - 1 + y3 + 3xy
This can be written in the form
x3 - 1 + y3 + 3xy = (x)2 + (-1)3 + (y)3 - 3(x).(-1).(y)
Recall the formula a3 + b3 + c3 - 3abc = (a + b + c) (a2 + b2 + c2 - ab - bc - ca)
Using the above formula, we have,
x3 - 1 + y3 + 3xy
= (x + (-1) + y} {(x)2 + (-1)2 + (y)2 - (x).(-1) - (-1).(y) - (y).(x)}
= (x - 1 + y)(x2 + 1 + y2 + x + y - xy)

questions.question: If (x + y)3 - ( x - y )3 - 6y (x2 - y2) = ky3, then k =
questions.explanation:
We have,
= (x + y)3 - (x - y)3 - 6y(x2 - y2) = ky3
= (x + y - x + y)3 + 3(x + y)(x - y) (x + y - x + y) - 6y(x2 - y2) = ky3
= 2y3 + 6y(x2 - y2) - 6y(x2 - y2) = ky3
= 8y3 = ky3
= k = 8

questions.question: The factors of x3 - x2y - xy2 + y3 , are
questions.explanation:
The given expression to be factorized is x3 - x2y - xy2 + y3
Take common x2 from the first two terms and -y2 from the last two terms. That is
x3 - x2y - xy2 + y3 = x2(x - y) - y2(x - y)
Finally, take common (x - y) from the two terms. That is
x3 - x2y - xy2 + y3 = (x - y)(x2 - y2)
= (x - y){(x2 - y2)}
= (x - y)(x + y(x - y)
= (x - y)2(x + y)

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{(2.3)^{3}-0.027}{(2.3)^{2}+0.69+0.09}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
The given expression is
{tex}\frac{(2.3)^{3}-0.027}{(2.3)^{2}+0.69+0.09}{/tex}
This can be written in the form
{tex}\frac{(2.3)^{3}-(0.3)^{3}}{(2.3)^{2}+2.3 \times 0.3+(0.3)^{2}}{/tex}
Assume a = 2.3 and b = 0.3. Then the given expression can be rewritten as
{tex}\frac{a^{3}-b^{3}}{a^{2}+a b+b^{2}}{/tex}
Recall the formula for difference of two cubes
a3 - b3 = (a - b)(a2 + ab + b2)
Using the above formula, the expression becomes
{tex}\frac{(a-b)\left(a^{2}+a b+b^{2}\right)}{a^{2}+a b+b^{2}}{/tex}
Note that both a and b are positive, unequal. So, neither a3 -b3 nor any factor of it can be zero.
Therefore we can cancel the term (a2 + ab + b2) from both numerator and denominator. Then the expression becomes
{tex}\frac{(a-b)\left(a^{2}+a b+b^{2}\right)}{a^{2}+a b+b^{2}}=a-b{/tex}
= 2.3 - 0.3
= 2

questions.question: (x + y)3 - (x - y)3 can be factorized as
questions.explanation:
put a=x+y and b=x-y,then
{tex}(x+y)^3-(x-y)^3=a^3-b^3{/tex}
{tex}=(a-b)(a^2+b^2+ab){/tex}
{tex}=(x+y-x+y)[(x+y)^2+(x-y)^2+(x-y)(x+y)]{/tex} 
{tex}=2y[2(x^2+y^2)+(x^2-y^2)]{/tex}
{tex}=2y[3x^2+y^2]{/tex}

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{(0.013)^{3}+(0.007)^{3}}{(0.013)^{2}-0.013 \times 0.007+(0.007)^{2}}, \text { is }{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Assume a = 0.013 and b = 0.007. Then the given expression can be rewritten as
{tex}\frac{a^3 + b^{3}}{a^{2}-a b+b^{2}}{/tex}
Recall the formula for sum of two cubes
a3 + b3 = (a + b)(a2 - ab + b2)
Using the above formula, the expression becomes
{tex}\frac{(a+b)\left(a^{2}-a b+b^{2}\right)}{a^{2}-a b+b^{2}}{/tex}
Note that both a and b are positive. So, neither a3 + b3 nor any factor of it can be zero.
Therefore we can cancel the term (a2 - ab + b2) from both numerator and denominator. Then the expression becomes
{tex}\frac{(a+b)\left(a^{2}-a b+b^{2}\right)}{a^{2}-a b+b^{2}}=a+b{/tex}
= 0.013 + 0.007
= 0.02

questions.question: The factors of a2 - 1 - 2x - x2, are
questions.explanation:
The given expression to be factorized is a2 - 1 - 2x - x2
Take common -1 from the last three terms and then we have
a2 - 1 - 2x - x2 = a2 - (1 + 2x + x3)
= a2 - {(1)2 + 2.1.x + (x)2}
= a2 - (1 + x)2
= (a)2 - (1 + x)2
= {a + (1 + x)}{a - (1 + x)}
= (a + 1 + x)(a - 1 - x)
= (a + x + 1)(a - x - 1)

questions.question: The expression x4 + 4 can be factorized as

= (x2 + 2)2 - (2x)2
= (x2 + 2 + 2x) (x2 + 2 - 2x)

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{a}{b}+\frac{b}{a}=1{/tex}, then a3 + b3 =

questions.question: If a + b + c = 9 and ab + bc + ca = 23, then a3 + b3 + c3 - 3abc =

questions.question: If a1/3 + b1/3 + c1/3 = 0, then
questions.explanation:

questions.question: If a2 + b2 + c2 - ab -bc - ca = 0, then
questions.explanation:
Given:  a2 + b2 + c2 - ab - bc- ca = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(a2 + b2 + c2- ab - bc - ca) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2a2 + 2b2 + 2c2 - 2ab - 2bc - 2ca) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ({a2 + b2 - 2ab} + {b2 + c2 - 2bc} + {c2 + a2 - 2ca}) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - b)2 + (b - c)2 + (c - a)2 = 0
Now, since the sum of all squares is zero
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a - b = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = b
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b - c = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = c
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}c - a = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} c = a
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = b = c

questions.question: If {tex}3 x+\frac{2}{x}=7{/tex}, then {tex}\left(9 x^{2}-\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right){/tex}=
questions.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 2)(3x - 1) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=2 \text { or } x=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} (neglected)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}3 x-\frac{2}{x}=6-1=5{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}3 x+\frac{2}{x}=6+1=7{/tex}
Now,
{tex}(3 x)^{2}-\left(\frac{2}{x}\right)^{2}{/tex}{tex}=\left(3 x-\frac{2}{x}\right)\left(3 x+\frac{2}{x}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(3 x)^{2}-\left(\frac{2}{x}\right)^{2}{/tex} = 5(7) = 35

questions.question: If {tex}x-\frac{1}{x}=\frac{15}{4}{/tex}, then {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:

Now , {tex}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{2}=\left(\frac{15}{4}\right)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x^{2}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)-2{/tex}{tex}\times x \times \frac{1}{x}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{225}{16}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x^{2}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right){/tex}{tex}=\frac{257}{16}{/tex}
 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(x^{2}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)+2{/tex}{tex}\times x \times \frac{1}{x}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{257}{16}+2{/tex}{tex}\times x \times \frac{1}{x}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}x^{4}+\frac{1}{x^{4}}=194{/tex}, then {tex}x^{3}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(x^{4 }+\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)=194{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(x^{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right){/tex}{tex}+2 \times x^{2} \times \frac{1}{x^{2}}=194{/tex}{tex}+2 \times x^{2} \times \frac{1}{x^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{2}=196{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}{/tex}{tex}=\sqrt{196}=14{/tex}
Now,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x^{2}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)+2{/tex}{tex}\times x \times \frac{1}{x}=14{/tex}{tex}+2 \times x \times \frac{1}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{2}=16{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex}{tex}=\sqrt{16}=4{/tex}
Now, {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{3}=(4)^{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}(x)^{3}+\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^{3}+3{/tex}{tex}\times x \times \frac{1}{x}{/tex}{tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)=64{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x^{3}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)+3(4)=64{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left(x^{3}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)=64{/tex} - 12 = 52

questions.question: If {tex}x^{4}+\frac{1}{x^{4}}=623{/tex}, then {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex} =

Now ,

questions.question: If {tex}x^{3}-\frac{1}{x^{3}}=14{/tex}, then {tex}x-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
Given : {tex}x^{3}-\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)=14{/tex}
Let x = a and {tex}\frac{1}{x}=b{/tex}
Say, {tex}x-\frac{1}{x}=A{/tex}
Then, a3 - b3 = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - b)(a2 + ab + b2) = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - b)( {(a - b)2 + 2ab} + 2ab) = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - b){(a-b)2 + 3ab} = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a - b){(a - b)2 + 3} = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A(A2 + 3) = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A(A2 + 3) = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A3 + 3A - 14 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A3 - 2A2 + 2A2 - 4A + 7 A - 14 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A2(A - 2) + 2Y(Y - 2) + 7(Y - 2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (A-2)(A2+ 2A + 7) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A - 2 = 0,   {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x-\frac{1}{x}=2{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}x^{3}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}=110{/tex}, then {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{3}{/tex} {tex}=110+3\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{3}{/tex}{tex}-3\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)-110=0{/tex}
Let {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}=a{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a3 - 3a -110=0

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a=5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}=5{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}=102{/tex}, then {tex}x-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} =

questions.question: If {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}=3{/tex}, then {tex}x^{6}+\frac{1}{x^{6}}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
On cubing we get
{tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{3}{/tex}{tex}=x^{3}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right){/tex}{tex}+3 . x \cdot \frac{1}{x}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}27=x^{3}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)+3.3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x^{3}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)=27-9{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x^{3}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)=18{/tex}
Now, {tex}\left(x^{3}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{2}{/tex} {tex}=x^{6}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{6}}\right)+2 \cdot x^{3} \cdot \frac{1}{x^{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 182 = x6+ {tex}\left(\frac{1}{x^{6}}\right)+2{/tex}
{tex}x^{6}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{6}}\right){/tex} = 324 - 2 = 322

questions.question: If {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}=4{/tex}, then {tex}x^{4}+\frac{1}{x^{4}}{/tex}=
questions.explanation:
Using, (a+b)2= a2 + b2 + 2ab
{tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{2}{/tex} = {tex}x^{2}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right){/tex} + {tex}2 \cdot x \frac{1}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (4)2 = x2 + {tex}\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)+2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}{/tex} = 16 -2
{tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}=14{/tex}
Again, squaring both sides,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{2}{/tex} = (14)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x^{4}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right){/tex} + {tex}2 x^{2} \cdot \frac{1}{x^{2}}=196{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x^{4}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right){/tex} = 196 - 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x^{4}+\left(\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)=194{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}=2{/tex} , then {tex}x^{3}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:

questions.question: If {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}=5{/tex}, then {tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}={/tex}

{tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}{/tex} = 23

questions.question: If x3 - 3x2 + 3x - 7 = (x + 1)(ax2 +bx + c), then a + b + c =
questions.explanation:
First multiply
(x + 1)(ax² + bx + c)
= ax³ + bx² + cx + ax² + bx + c
= ax³ + bx² + ax² + cx + bx + c
= ax³ + (b+a)x² + (c + b)x + c
Comparing it with
x³ - 3x² + 3x - 7
a = 1
b + a = -3 => b + 1 = -3 => b = -4
c + b = 3 = > c - 4 = 3 => c = 7
c = -7 should be 7
as if we put x = -1 in
x³ - 3x² + 3x - 7
-1 - 3 - 3 - 7 = 14 so x + 1 can not be factor so x +1 will be factor if
x³ - 3x² + 3x - 7 is actually
x³ - 3x² + 3x + 7
then -1 - 3 - 3 + 7 = 0 
hence we can say that
a = 1
b = -4
c = 7
so a + b + c = 4
