questions.question: If the area of a sector of a circle is {tex}\frac{7}{20}{/tex} of the area of the circle, then the angle at the centre is equal to
questions.explanation:
We have given that area of the sector is {tex}\frac{7}{20}{/tex} of the area of the circle.
Therefore, area of the sector {tex}=\frac{7}{20} \times{/tex}area of the circle
{tex}\therefore \frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi r^{2}=\frac{7}{20} \times \pi r^{2}{/tex}
Now we will simplify the equation as below,
{tex}\frac{\theta}{360}=\frac{7}{20}{/tex}
Now we will multiply both sides of the equation by 360,
{tex}\therefore \theta=\frac{7}{20} \times 360{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \theta=126{/tex}
Therefore, sector angle is {tex}126^{\circ}{/tex}.

questions.question: The area of a sector whose perimeter is four times its radius r units, is
questions.explanation:
Radius of sector = r
Perimeter = 4r
and length of arc = 4r - 2r = 2r
 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Let angle at the centre = {tex}\theta{/tex}
Then, {tex}2 \pi r=\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}{/tex} and {tex}2r = \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \pi \times \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}{/tex} = 1 ...(i)
Now area = {tex}\pi r^{2} \times \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}=r^{2}\left(\pi \times \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}\right){/tex}
= r2 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 [from (i)]
= r2

questions.question: If a chord of a circle of radius 28 cm makes an angle of 90o at the centre, then the area of the major segment is
questions.explanation:
A chord AB makes an angle of 90o at the centre
Radius of the circle = 28 cm

Area of minor segment ACB
{tex}=\pi r^{2} \times \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}{/tex} - area of {tex}\triangle AOB{/tex}
{tex}=\pi r^{2} \times \frac{90^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}-\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O} \mathrm{A} \times \mathrm{OB}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{4} \pi r^{2}-\frac{1}{2} \times r^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 28 \times 28-\frac{1}{2} \times 28 \times 28{/tex}
= 616 - 392
= 224 cm2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of the major segment ADB
= Area of circle - area of minor segment
{tex}=\pi r^{2}-224=\frac{22}{7} \times 28 \times 28 - 224{/tex}
= 2464 - 224
= 2240 sq. cm

questions.question: The hour hand of a clock is 6 cm long. The area swept by it between 11.20 am and 11.55 am is
questions.explanation:
Length of hour hand of a clock (r) = 6 cm
Time 11.20 am to 11.55 am = 35 minute = {tex}\frac{35}{60} \mathrm{h}{/tex}
 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} In 1 hour the hour hand rotates {tex}30^{\circ}{/tex}.
Thus, central angle of the sector = {tex}30 \times \frac{35}{60}=17.5^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of the sector swept by the hour hand = {tex}\frac{17.5}{360} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 6 \times 6{/tex} cm2
 {tex}=\frac{2.5 \times 22}{10} \mathrm{cm}^{2}=5.5 \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The area of a sector of angle  θ0 of a circle with radius R is
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{\pi R^{2} \theta}{360}{/tex}

questions.question: The length of the minute hand of a clock is 21 cm. The area swept by the minute hand in 10 minutes is
questions.explanation:
Area swept by minute hand in 60 minutes = {tex}\pi R^{2}{/tex}
Area swept by it in 10 minutes
{tex}=\left(\frac{\pi R^{2}}{60} \times 10\right) \mathrm{cm}^{2}=\left(\frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times 21 \times \frac{1}{6}\right) \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}
= 231 cm2

questions.question: A chord of a circle of radius 10 cm subtends a right angle at the centre. The area of the minor segments (given, {tex}\pi{/tex} = 3.14) is
questions.explanation:
ar(minor segment A C B A)=ar(sector  O A C B O) - {tex}\operatorname{ar}(\Delta O A B){/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{\pi r^{2} \theta}{360}-\frac{1}{2} \times r \times r\right){/tex}


{tex}=\left(\frac{3.14 \times 10 \times 10 \times 90}{360}-\frac{1}{2} \times 10 \times 10\right) \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=(78.5-50) \mathrm{cm}^{2}=28.5 \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: In a circle of radius 21 cm, an arc subtends an angle of 600 at the centre. The length of the arc is
questions.explanation: Arc length ={tex}\frac{2 \pi r \theta}{360}=\left(2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times \frac{60}{360}\right) \mathrm{cm}=22 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}

questions.question: In a circle of radius 14 cm, an arc subtends an angle of 1200 at the centre. If {tex}\sqrt3{/tex}= 1.73 then the area of the segment of the circle is
questions.explanation:
ar(segment) = {tex}\left(\frac{\pi r^{2} \theta}{360}-r^{2} \sin \frac{\theta}{2} \cos \frac{\theta}{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{22}{7} \times 14 \times 14 \times \frac{120}{360}\right)-\left(14 \times 14 \times \sin 60^{\circ} \cos 60^{\circ}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{616}{3}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} \times 14 \times 14\right) \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}
=(205.33  -49{tex} \times {/tex}1.73) cm2
=(205.33 - 84.77) cm2
= 120.56 cm2

questions.question: The area of a sector of a circle with radius r, making an angle of x° at the centre is
questions.explanation:
Area of a sector of a circle with radius r and making an angle of {tex}x^{0}{/tex}
at the centre = {tex}\frac{x}{360} \times \pi r^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The length of an arc of a sector of angle {tex}\theta{/tex}° of a circle with radius R is
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{2 \pi R \theta}{360}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle (in degrees) of a sector of a circle of radius r, then area of the sector is
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{\pi r^{2} \theta}{360}{/tex}

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 35 cm, 54 cm and 61 cm, respectively. The length of its longest altitude
questions.explanation:
Let ABC be a triangle in which sides AB = 35cm, BC = 54 cm and CA = 61 cm

Now, semi-perimeter of a triangle,
{tex}s=\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{35+54+61}{2}=\frac{150}{2}=75 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
[{tex}\because{/tex} semiperimeter, s = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}]
{tex}\because{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC {tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex} [by Heron's formula]
{tex}=\sqrt{75(75-35)(75-54)(75-61)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{75 \times 40 \times 21 \times 14}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{25 \times 3 \times 4 \times 2 \times 5 \times 7 \times 3 \times 7 \times 2}{/tex}
{tex}=5 \times 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 7 \sqrt{5}{/tex}
{tex}=420 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}
Also, Area of {tex}\triangle A B C=\frac{1}{2} \times A B \times \text { Altitude }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \times 35 \times C D{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow C D=\frac{420 \times 2 \sqrt{5}}{35}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} CD {tex}=24 \sqrt{5}{/tex}
Hence, the length of altitude is {tex}24 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}.

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 16 cm, 30 cm, 34 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation:
Let a = 16 cm, b = 30 cm, c = 34 cm
Semi-perimeter of a triangle = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}=\frac{16+30+34}{2}{/tex} = 40
Now, s - a = 24 cm, s - b = 10 cm and s - c = 6 cm
By Heron's formula. Area of a triangle = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{40 \times 24 \times 10 \times 6}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{4 \times 10 \times 4 \times 6 \times 10 \times 6} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{4^{2} \times 10^{2} \times 6^{2}} {/tex}
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
= 240 cm2

questions.question: The base of an isosceles right triangle is 30 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation:
Since triangle ABC is an isosceles triangle.
Base = 30
Hence, height = 30 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of triangle ABC {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times A B \times B C=\frac{1}{2} \times 30 \times 30=450 \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 7 cm, 9 cm and 14 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation:
Let a = 7 cm, b = 9 cm, c = 14 cm
Semi-perimeter = s = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}=\frac{7+9+14}{2}{/tex} = 15 cm
s - a = 15 - 7 = 8cm, s - b = 15 - 9 = 6 cm and s - c = 15 - 14 = 1 cm
Are of a triangle = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{15 \times 8 \times 6 \times 1}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{5 \times 3 \times 4 \times 2 \times 3 \times 2}{/tex}
= {tex}12 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 325 m, 300 m and 125 m. Its area is
questions.explanation:
a = 325 m, b = 300 m, c = 125 m
s = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}=\frac{325+300+125}{2}{/tex} = 375 m
s - a = 50 m, s - b = 75 m , s - c = 250 m
Area = {tex}\sqrt{s}(s-a)(s-b)(s-c){/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{375 \times 50 \times 75 \times 250}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{15 \times 25 \times 25 \times 2 \times 3 \times 25 \times 25 \times 10}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{25 \times 25 \times 25 \times 25 \times 30 \times 30}{/tex}
= 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 30
= 18750 m2

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 11 m, 60 m and 61 m. The altitude to the smallest side is
questions.explanation:
Area of {tex}\Delta=\frac{1}{2} \text { Base } \times \text { Height }{/tex}
The smallest side is 11 m
Area = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times 11 \times{/tex} Height .. (i)
Area by Heron's Formula = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
s {tex}\frac{11+60+61}{2}{/tex} = 66 m
Area = {tex}\sqrt{66 \times 55 \times 6 \times 5}{/tex} = 330 m2
From eq (i)
330 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times 11 \times{/tex} height
Height = {tex}\frac{2 \times 330}{11}{/tex} = 60 m

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 11 cm, 15 cm and 16 cm. The altitude to the largest side is
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}\frac{11+15+16}{2}{/tex} = 21 cm
Area of {tex}=\Delta=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{21 \times 10 \times 6 \times 5}=30 \sqrt{7} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}
Also if we choose largest side and its Altitude, the area would be
A = {tex}\frac{1}{2} {/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} largest side {tex}\times{/tex} h
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \times 16 \times h=30 \sqrt{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow h=\frac{30 \sqrt{7}}{8}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{15 \sqrt{7}}{4}{/tex}cm

questions.question: The base and hypotenuse of a right triangle are respectively 5 cm and 13 cm long. it is area is:
questions.explanation:
AB = {tex}\sqrt{(13)^{2}-(5)^{2}}={/tex} 12 cm
Area = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times \mathrm{BC} \times \mathrm{AB}=\frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 12{/tex}
= 30 cm2

questions.question: The lengths of the sides of {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABCare consecutive integers. It {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC has perimeter as an equilateral triangle triangle with a side of length 9 cm, what is the length of the shortest side of {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC?
questions.explanation:
Let the sides of {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC be n, n + 1, n + 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Perimeter = n + n + 1 + n + 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}(9 + 9 + 9) = 3n + 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 27 = 3n + 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3n = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 8 cm
Thus, the shortest side is 8 cm

questions.question: If the length of a median of an equilateral triangle is x cm, then its area, is
questions.explanation:
Let the side of equilateral {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC be a cm
The median of equilateral triangle is its altitude drawn from A to BC. (i.e. the height of {tex}\Delta{/tex} over Base BC)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AD = a sin 60o
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{a \sqrt{3}}{2}{/tex} [AD = x (given)
{tex}\Rightarrow a=\frac{2 x}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
Area of equilateral {tex}\Delta{/tex} of side a = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3} a^{2}}{4}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\left(\frac{2 x}{\sqrt{3}}\right)^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}

questions.question: A square and an equilateral triangle have equal perimeters. If the diagonal of the square is 12{tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} cm, then area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
If side of a square is a cm then, its diagonal = {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} a cm
But diagonal = 12{tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} cm
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{2}{/tex} a = 12{tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 12 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Perimeter of a square = 4a = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 12= 48cm
Now, perimeter of an equilateral triangle with side x = 3x cm
But, perimeter of equilateral triangle = Perimeter of square
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 48
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 16 cm
Now, Area of equilateral {tex}\Delta=\frac{\sqrt{3} x^{2}}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 16 = 64{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} cm2

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 50 cm, 78 cm and 112 cm. The smallest altitude is
questions.explanation:
The smallest altitude is {tex}\perp{/tex} drawn to the largest side of a {tex}\Delta{/tex} from opposite point.
i.e. BD Area of {tex}\Delta=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Ac {tex}\times{/tex} BD = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 112 {tex}\times{/tex} BD = 56 {tex}\times{/tex}BD
s = {tex}\frac{50+78+112}{2}{/tex} = 120cm
s - AB = 70 cm, s - BC = 42 cm, s - AC = 8 cm
Area = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-A B)-(s-B C)(s-A C)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{120 \times 70 \times 42 \times 8}{/tex}
= 1680 cm2
Now, 56{tex}\times{/tex}BD = 1680 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BD= {tex}\frac{1680}{56}{/tex} = 30 cm

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, it is given that base = 12 cm and height = 5 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation:
30 cm2
Area of triangle {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times 12 \times 5{/tex}
= 30 cm2

questions.question: Each side of an equilateral triangle measures 8 cm. The area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle  {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times(\text { Side })^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times(8)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 64{/tex}
{tex}=16 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The base of an isosceles triangle is 8 cm long and each of its equal sides measures 6 cm. The area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Area of isosceles triangle {tex}=\frac{b}{4} \sqrt{4 a^{2}-b^{2}}{/tex}
Here,
a = 6 cm and b = 8 cm
Thus, we have
{tex}\frac{8}{4} \times \sqrt{4(6)^{2}-8^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{4} \times \sqrt{144-64}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{4} \times \sqrt{80}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{4} \times 4 \sqrt{5}{/tex}
{tex}=8 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The base of an isosceles triangle is 6 cm and each of its equal sides is 5 cm. The height of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Height of isosceles triangle {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{4 a^{2}-b^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{4(5)^{2}-6^{2}}{/tex} (a = 5 cm and b = 6 cm)
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times \sqrt{100-36}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times \sqrt{64}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times 8{/tex}
= 4 cm

questions.question: Each of the two equal sides of an isosceles right triangle is 10 cm long. Its area is
questions.explanation:
Here, the base and height of the triangle are 10 cm and 10 cm, respectively.
Thus, we have
Area of triangle {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times 10 \times 10{/tex}
= 50 cm2

questions.question: Each side of an equilateral triangle is 10 cm long. The height of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Height of equilateral triangle {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times{/tex} Side
{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 10{/tex}
{tex}=5 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}

questions.question: The lengths of the three sides of a triangular field are 40 m, 24 m and 32 m respectively. The area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Let:
a = 40 m, b = 24 m and c = 32 m
 S {tex}=\frac{40+24+32}{2}{/tex} = 48 m
By Heron's formula, we have:
Area of triangle {tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{48(48-40)(48-24)(48-32)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{48 \times 8 \times 24 \times 16}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{24 \times 2 \times 8 \times 24 \times 8 \times 2}{/tex}
{tex}=24 \times 8 \times 2{/tex}
= 384 m2

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are in the ratio 5 : 12 : 13 and its perimeter is 150 cm. The area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Let the sides of the triangle be 5x cm, 12x cm and 13x cm.
Perimeter = Sum of all sides
or, 150 = 5x + 12x + 13x
or, 30x = 150
or, x = 5
Thus, the sides of the triangle are 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 cm, 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 cm and 13 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 cm, i.e., 25 cm, 60 cm and 65 cm.
Now,
Let:
a = 25 cm, b = 60 cm and c = 65 cm
{tex}s=\frac{150}{2}=75 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
By Heron's formula, we have:
Area of triangle {tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{75(75-25)(75-60)(75-65)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{75 \times 50 \times 15 \times 10}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{15 \times 5 \times 5 \times 10 \times 15 \times 10}{/tex}
{tex}=15 \times 5 \times 10{/tex}
= 750 cm2

questions.question: The lengths of the three sides of a triangle are 30 cm, 24 cm and 18 cm respectively. The length of the altitude of the triangle corresponding to the smallest side is
questions.explanation:
Let:
a = 30 cm, b = 24 cm and c = 18 cm
{tex}s=\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{30+24+18}{2}=36 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
On applying Heron's formula, we get:
Area of triangle {tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{36(36-30)(36-24)(36-18)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{36 \times 6 \times 12 \times 18}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{12 \times 3 \times 12 \times 6 \times 3}{/tex}
{tex}=12 \times 3 \times 6{/tex}
= 216 cm2
The smallest side is 18 cm.
Hence, the altitude of the triangle corresponding to 18 cm is given by:
Area of triangle = 216 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height = 216 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height {tex}=\frac{216 \times 2}{18}{/tex} = 24 cm

questions.question: The area of an equilateral triangle is {tex}81 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}. Its height is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle {tex}=81 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times(\text { Side })^{2}=81 \sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Side)2 {tex}=81 \times 4{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Side)2 = 324
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Side = 18 cm
Now,
Height {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times Side{/tex}{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 18=9 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}

questions.question: The base of an isosceles triangle is 16 cm and its area is 48 cm2. The perimeter of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR be an isosceles triangle and PX {tex}\perp{/tex} QR
Now,
Area of triangle = 48 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \times Q R \times P X{/tex} = 48
{tex}\Rightarrow h=\frac{96}{16}=6 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
Also,
{tex}Q X=\frac{1}{2} \times 24{/tex} = 12 cm and PX = 12 cm
{tex}P Q=\sqrt{Q X^{2}+P X^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}a=\sqrt{8^{2}+6^{2}}=\sqrt{64+36}{/tex}{tex}=\sqrt{100}=10 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Perimeter  = (10 + 10 + 16) cm = 36 cm

questions.question: The base of a triangle is 12cm and height is 8cm then area of triangle is
questions.explanation:
Area of triangle =  {tex}\frac {1}{2} \times base \times height{/tex}
{tex}= \frac {1}{2} \times 12 \times 8 = 48{/tex}

questions.question: An isosceles right triangle has area 8 cm2 The length of its hypotenuse is
questions.explanation:
Given, area of an isosceles right triangle = 8cm2
Area of an isosceles triangle {tex}= \frac {1}{2} (Base \times Height){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 8 = \frac {1}{2} (Base \times Base){/tex}
[{tex}\therefore{/tex} Base = height, as triangle is an isosceles triangle]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Base)2 = 16 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base = 4 cm

In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, using Pythagoras theorem
Ac2 = AB2 + BC2 = 42 + 42 = 16 + 16
{tex}\Rightarrow A C^{2}=32 \Rightarrow A C=\sqrt{32} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
[taking positive square root because length is always positive]
Hence, the length of its hypotenuse is {tex}\sqrt{32} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}.

questions.question: A piece of paper in the shape of a sector of a circle (see figure 1) is rolled up to form a right-circular cone (see figure 2). The value of angle {tex}\theta{/tex} is :
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{10 \pi}{13}{/tex}

questions.question: A piece of paper in the shape of a sector of a circle (see figure 1) is rolled up to form a right-circular cone (see figure 2). The value of angle {tex}\theta{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Slant height = 13
As, {tex}\theta=\frac{S}{r}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S = r{tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\pi{/tex}(5) = 13 {tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta=\frac{10 \pi}{13}{/tex}

questions.question: Find the total distance travelled by Mohit through the motorbike in a week to go to college.
questions.explanation:
In a week, Mohit drives his motorbike 3 days to go to college
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total distance travelled by Mohit through motorbike = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 50.4 km

questions.question: Find the total distance travelled by Mohit through the bicycle in a week to go to college.
questions.explanation:
In a week Mohit rides his bicycle 3 days to go to college.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total distance travelled by Mohit through bicycle
= Length of Arc {tex}\widehat{A C B} \times 6{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times 2 \pi r \times{/tex} 6 = {tex}\frac{60^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times {/tex} 2 {tex}\times \frac{22}{7} \times {/tex} 4.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 26.4 km

questions.question: Find the area of sector AOB.
questions.explanation:
Area of sector AOB = {tex}\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{60^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \frac{22}{7} \times{/tex} (4.2)2 = 9.24 km2

questions.question: If the cost of fuel for the motorbike is ₹ 20 per km, then find the total cost of fuel used in a week in going to college.
questions.explanation:
Total cost of fuel used for a week
= ₹(20 {tex}\times{/tex} 50.4) = ₹ 1008

questions.question: If the angle of sector changed from 60° to 90°, then find the total length of the available paths.
questions.explanation:
Total length of available paths
= 4.2 + 4.2 + {tex}\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times 2 \pi r{/tex}
= {tex}8.4+\frac{90^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 4.2{/tex} = = 8.4 + 6.6 = 15 km

questions.question: What is the length of equal sides?
questions.explanation:
Let x cm be the length of equal sides of the isosceles triangle.
So, x + x + 4 = 20
2x + 4 = 20
2x = 20 - 4
2x = 16
x = {tex}\frac{16}{2}{/tex} = 8 cm

questions.question: What is the Heron's formula for the area of?
questions.explanation: {tex}|\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}|{/tex}

questions.question: What is the semi perimeter of the highlighted triangle?
questions.explanation:
Required semi perimeter = {tex}\frac{Perimeter}{2}=\frac{20}{2}{/tex}
= 10 cm

questions.question: What is the area of highlighted triangle?
questions.explanation:
Since, semi perimeter,
s = 10 cm
Thus, area of the triangle
{tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}=\sqrt{10(10-8)(10-8)(10-4)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{10(2)(2)(6)}{/tex}
= {tex}4 \sqrt{15}{/tex} cm2

questions.question: If the sides of a triangle are in the ratio 3 : 5 : 7 and its perimeter is 300 m. Find its area.
questions.explanation:
Let the sides of a triangle are
a = 3x, b = 5x, c = 7x
then a + b + c = 300
3x + 5x + 7x = 300
15x = 300
x = 20
So, a = 60, b = 100, c = 140
s = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{300}{2}{/tex}
= 150
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{150(150-60)(150-100)(150-140)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{150 \times 90 \times 50 \times 10}{/tex}
= {tex}1500 \sqrt{3}{/tex} m2

questions.explanation:
Length of RQ = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of BC

= 14 cm

questions.question: If colourful rope is to be placed along the sides of small {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR. What is the length of the rope?
questions.explanation:
Length of rope = Perimeter of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR
= (12.5 + 13 + 14) cm
= 39.5 cm

questions.question:
Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR =
where s is the semi-perimeter of {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR.

questions.question: Find the area swept by the minute hand in 10 minutes.
questions.explanation:
Angle made by minute hand in 60 minutes = 360o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Angle made by minute hand in 10 minutes = {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{60} \times 10=60^{\circ}{/tex}
Length of minute hand = 9 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area swept by minute hand in 10 minutes = Area of sector having central angle 60o
= {tex}\pi r^{2}\left(\frac{60^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}\right)=\frac{22}{7} \times 9 \times 9 \times \frac{1}{6}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{297}{7}{/tex} = 42.42 cm2

questions.question: If the pendulum covers a distance of 22 cm in one complete oscillation, then find the angle described by pendulum at the centre.
questions.explanation:
We have, r = 15 cm and l = {tex}\frac{1}{2}(22){/tex} = 11 cm
We known that l = {tex}2 \pi r\left(\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta=\frac{11 \times 360^{\circ}}{2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 15}=\frac{90^{\circ} \times 7}{15}=6^{\circ} \times{/tex} 7 = 42°

questions.question: Find the angle described by hour hand in 10 minutes.
questions.explanation:
Angle made by hour hand in 12 hours = 360°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Angle made by hour hand in 10 minutes = {tex}\left(\frac{360^{\circ}}{12} \times \frac{1}{6}\right)=5^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: Find the area swept by the hour hand in 1 hour.
questions.explanation:
Angle made by hour hand in 1 hour = {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{12}=30^{\circ}{/tex}
Also, r = 6 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area swept by hour hand in 1 hour = Area of sector having central angle 30°
= {tex}\pi r^{2} \times\left(\frac{30^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}\right)=\frac{22}{7} \times 6 \times 6 \times \frac{1}{12}=\frac{66}{7}{/tex} = 9.428 cm2

questions.question: Find the area swept by the hour hand between 11 a.m. and 5 p.m.
questions.explanation:
Number of hours from 11 a.m. to 5 p.m. = 6
Area swept by hour hand in 1 hour = 9.428 cm2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area swept by hour hand in 6 hours = 9.428 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
= 56.568 cm2

questions.question: An equilateral triangle of side 12 cm is inscribed in a circle, then the radius of the circle is
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\triangle{/tex} ABC be an equilateral triangle of side 12 cm.

Let AD be one of its median, then
AD {tex}\perp{/tex} BC and BD = {tex}\frac{12}{2}{/tex} = 6 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AD = {tex}\sqrt{A B^{2}-B D^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{(12)^{2}-(6)^{2}}{/tex} [using Pythagoras theorem in right angled {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADB]
= {tex}\sqrt{144-36}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{108}{/tex} = 6{tex} \sqrt{3}{/tex}
Let G be the centroid of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, then AG : GD = 2 : 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius = AG = {tex}\frac23{/tex} AD = {tex}\frac23{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 6{tex} \sqrt{3}{/tex} = 4{tex} \sqrt{3}{/tex} cm

questions.question: If AOB is a diameter of a circle with centre O such that AB = 50 cm and CD is a chord of length 48 cm, then distance of CD from AB is
questions.explanation:
Here, OP {tex}\perp{/tex} CD

{tex}\therefore{/tex} CP = {tex}\frac12{/tex} CD = {tex}\frac12{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 48 = 24 cm
Radius of circle, OC = {tex}\frac{AB}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{50}{2}{/tex} = 25 cm
Now, OP = {tex}\sqrt{O C^{2}-C P^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{(25)^{2}-(24)^{2}}{/tex} [using Pythagoras theorem in right angled {tex}\triangle{/tex}OPC]
= {tex}\sqrt{625-576}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{49}{/tex} = 7

questions.question: In a circle of radius 5 cm, AB and AC are two chords such that AB = AC = 6 cm. Find the length of the chord BC.
questions.explanation:
Let O be the centre of the circle.
Let the bisector of {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC meet the chord BC at M.

In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BAM and {tex}\triangle{/tex}CAM,
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAM = {tex}\angle{/tex}MAC [by construction]
AB = AC [given]
AM = AM [common side]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}BAM {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}CAM [by SAS congruence rule]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} BM = MC and {tex}\angle{/tex}BMA = {tex}\angle{/tex}AMC [by CPCT]
But {tex}\angle{/tex}BMA + {tex}\angle{/tex}AMC = 180o [{tex}\because{/tex} BMC is a straight line]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BMA = 90o
Therefore, AM is perpendicular bisector of BC and hence it passes through the centre O of the circle.
Let BM = y cm and OM = x cm, then
MA = OA  - OM = (5 - x) cm [{tex}\because{/tex} radius = 5 cm]
In right angled {tex}\triangle{/tex}OMB, y2 + x2 = 52 ...(i)
In right angled {tex}\triangle{/tex}BMA, y2 + (5 - x)2 = 62
i.e. y2 + x2 - 10x = 11 ...(ii) [by Pythagoras theorem]
On subtracting Eq. (ii) from Eq. (i), we get
10x = 25 - 11 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x = 14 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac75{/tex}
On substituting the value of x in Eq. (i), we get
y2 + {tex}\frac{19}{25}{/tex} = 25 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y2 = 25 - {tex}\frac{49}{25}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{625-49}{25}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y2 = {tex}\frac{576}{25}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = {tex}\frac{24}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Length of chord BC = 2BM = {tex}2 \cdot \frac{24}{5}{/tex} cm = {tex}\frac{48}{5}{/tex} cm = 9.6 cm

subtopics.name: Areas of Sector and Segment of a Circle (X-11.1)

questions.question: If two circles intersect in two points, then the line through their centres to the bisector of common chord is
questions.explanation:
Given that the two circles with centres C, D and intersecting at points A, B so that AB is their common chord.

In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ACD and {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD
CA = CB [radii of same circle]
AD = BD [radii of same circle]
CD = CD [common side]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}ACD {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD [by SSS congruence rule]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ACM = {tex}\angle{/tex}MCB [by CPCT]
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ACM and {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCM,
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACM = {tex}\angle{/tex}MCB
CA = CB [radii of same circle]
CM = CM [common side]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}ACM {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCM [by SAS congruence rule]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AM = MB and {tex}\angle{/tex}CMA = {tex}\angle{/tex}CMB [by CPCT]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CMA + {tex}\angle{/tex}CMB = 180o [{tex}\because{/tex} AMB is a straight line]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CMA = 90o [{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CMA = {tex}\angle{/tex}CMB]
Hence, CD is perpendicular bisector of AB.

questions.question: Two congruent circles intersect each other at points A and B. Through A, any line segment PAQ is drawn, so that P and Q lie on the two circles. Then,
questions.explanation:
Let O' and O be the centres of two congruent circles. Since, AB is a common chord of these circles.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BPA = {tex}\angle{/tex}BQA [since, angles subtended by equal chords are equal]

Hence, BP = BQ [{tex}\because{/tex} sides opposite to equal angles of a triangle are also equal]

questions.question: Two chords AB and CD of lengths 5 cm and 11 cm, respectively of a circle are parallel to each other and are on opposite sides of its centre. If the distance between AB and CD is 6 cm, then find the radius of the circle.
questions.explanation:
Let O be the centre of the given circle and its radius be b cm. Given, AB = 5 cm and CD = 11 cm are two chords on opposite sides of its centre. Draw ON {tex}\perp{/tex} AB and OM {tex}\perp{/tex} CD. Since, ON {tex}\perp{/tex} AB, OM {tex}\perp{/tex} CD and AB {tex}\parallel{/tex} CD, therefore the points N, O and M are collinear.

Given, distance between AB and.CD = MN = 6 cm
Let ON = a cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} OM = (6 - a) cm
Join OA and OC.
Then, OA = OC = b cm
We know that the perpendicular from the centre to a chord of the circle bisects the chord.
Then, AN = NB = {tex}\frac52{/tex} = 2.5 cm
and CM = MD = {tex}\frac{11}{2}{/tex} = 5.5 cm
In right angled {tex}\triangle{/tex}ONA and {tex}\triangle{/tex}OMC,
OA2 = ON2 + AN2
and OC2 = OM2 + CM2 [by Pythagoras theorem]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b2 = a2 + (2.5)2 ...(i)
and b2 = (6 - a)2 + (5.5)2 ...(ii)
So, a2 + (2.5)2 = (6 - a)2 + (5.5)2 [from Eqs. (i) and (ii)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 + 6.25 = 36 - 12a + a2 + 30.25
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12a = 60 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 5
On putting a = 5 in Eq. (i), we get
b2 = (5)2 + (2.5)2
= 25 + 6.25 = 31.25
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius = b = {tex}\sqrt{31.25}{/tex} = 5.6 cm (approx.).

questions.question: Area of one grey and red triangle is
questions.explanation:
For area of one grey and red triangle, sides are 4cm, 4 cm, 3 cm.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2s = 4 + 4 + 3 = 11 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} s = {tex}\frac{11}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} area of one grey and red triangle
{tex}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{2}\left(\frac{11}{2}-4\right)\left(\frac{11}{2}-4\right)\left(\frac{11}{2}-3\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{2} \cdot \frac{3}{2} \cdot \frac{3}{2} \cdot \frac{5}{2}}=\frac{3}{4} \sqrt{55} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of one red triangle is
questions.explanation:
For area of one red triangle, sides are 2 cm, 2 cm, {tex}\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2s = {tex}2+2+\frac{3}{2}=\frac{11}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow s=\frac{11}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} area {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{4}\left(\frac{11}{4}-2\right)\left(\frac{11}{4}-2\right)\left(\frac{11}{4}-\frac{3}{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{4} \cdot \frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{5}{4}}=\frac{3}{16} \sqrt{55} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of grey part is

area of grey part = 4(area of one grey part) {tex}=4\left(\frac{3}{4}-\frac{3}{16}\right) \sqrt{55} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(3-\frac{3}{4}\right) \sqrt{55}=\frac{9}{4} \sqrt{55} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Ratio of area of red part : area of grey part is
questions.explanation:
Ratio of red part : area of grey part
{tex}=\frac{3}{16} \sqrt{55}: \frac{9}{4} \sqrt{55}=\frac{3}{16}: \frac{9}{4}{/tex} = 3 : 36 = 1 : 12

questions.question: Area of portion I is
questions.explanation:
Portion I is an isosceles triangle with sides 1, 5, 5 [Base = 1]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2s = 1 + 5 + 5 = 11 {tex}\Rightarrow s=\frac{11}{2}{/tex}
Area of {tex}I=\sqrt{\frac{11}{2}\left(\frac{11}{2}-1\right)\left(\frac{11}{2}-5\right)\left(\frac{11}{2}-5\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{2} \times \frac{9}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{5.5 \times 4.5 \times 0.5 \times 0.5}=\sqrt{6.1875}{/tex}
= 2.5 cm2 (nearly)

questions.question: Area of portion II is
questions.explanation: Area of portion II = 6.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = 6.5 cm2

questions.question: Area of portion III is
questions.explanation:
Area of portion III = {tex}\frac{1}{2}(2+1){/tex} (height of the trapezium-III)
{tex}=\frac{3}{2}\left(\sqrt{1^{2}-(0.5)^{2}}\right)=\frac{3}{2} \sqrt{\frac{3}{4}}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{4}=\frac{3}{4}{/tex}(1.73) = 1.3 cm2 nearly

questions.question: Area of portion IV is
questions.explanation: Area of portion IV = {tex}\frac {1}{2}{/tex}(6)(1.5) = 4.5 cm2

questions.question: Area of portion V is
questions.explanation: Area of portion V = 4.5 cm2

questions.question: If the region containing blue colour makes an angle of 80° at the centre, then find the area of the region having blue colour.
questions.explanation:
Area of the region containing blue colour
= {tex}\frac{22}{7} \times 5 \times 5 \times \frac{80^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}-\frac{22}{7} \times 3 \times 3 \times \frac{80^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{22}{7} \times \frac{2}{9} \times[25-9]=\frac{44}{63}(16){/tex} = 11.17 cm2

questions.question: If the region containing green colour makes an angle of 60° at the centre, then find the area of the region having green colour.
questions.explanation:
Area of the region containing green colour
= {tex}\frac{22}{7} \times \frac{60^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}[5 \times 5-3 \times 3]=\frac{22}{7} \times \frac{1}{6} \times{/tex} 16 = 8.38 cm2

questions.question: If the region containing red colour makes an angle of 20° at the centre, then find the perimeter of the region containing red colour.
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of the region containing red colour = 2 + 2 + length of arc of sector having radius 3 cm + length of arc of sector having radius 5 cm
= 4 + 2 {tex}\times \frac{22}{7} \times 3 \times \frac{20^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}+2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 5 \times \frac{20^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}{/tex}
= 4 + {tex}\frac{44}{7} \times \frac{1}{18} \times 8=4+\frac{176}{63}{/tex} = 4 + 2.79 = 6.79 cm

questions.question: Find the area of the region having radius 3 cm.
questions.explanation: Required area = {tex}\frac{22}{7} \times 3 \times 3 \times \frac{160^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}=\frac{88}{7}{/tex} = 12.57 cm2

questions.question: The region given in the figure represents:
questions.explanation:
Angle of given sector = 80° + 60° + 20° = 160°
Thus, the given region represents minor sector of a circle.

questions.question: Area of wheat triangle is
questions.explanation:
For wheat, sides of the triangle ABC are 250 m, 350 m, 300 m
2s = 250 + 350 + 300 m = 900 m2
{tex}\therefore {/tex} s = 450 m
{tex}\therefore {/tex} area of this triangle = {tex}\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{450(450-250)(450-350)(450-300)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{450(200)(100)(150)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{1350000000}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{1350}(1000) \mathrm{m}^{2}{/tex} {tex}=15000 \sqrt{6} \mathrm{~m}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of potatoes and onions field is
questions.explanation:
For potatoes and onions, sides of triangle ADC are 440 m, 250 m, 330 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2s = 440 + 250 + 330 = 1020
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} s = 510
{tex}\therefore{/tex} required area = {tex}\sqrt{510(510-440)(510-250)(510-330)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{510(70)(260)(180)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{4641} \ 600 \mathrm{~m}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} area = {tex}\sqrt{1 s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of field of potatoes is
questions.explanation:
Area of field of potatoes = {tex}\frac{1}{2}(\sqrt{4641}) 600{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{4641}(300) \mathrm{m}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Area of field of onions is
questions.explanation: Area of field of onions = {tex}\sqrt{4641}(300) \mathrm{m}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Ratio of areas of potatoes and onions
questions.explanation: Required ratio = 1 : 1

questions.question: The area of the sector of a circle with radius 6 cm which subtends an angle of 60o at the centre of the circle is:
questions.explanation:
Angle of the sector is 60°
Area of sector = ({tex}\frac {\theta}{360^o}{/tex}) {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex}r2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of the sector with angle 60o = ({tex}\frac {60^o}{360^o}{/tex}) {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex}r2 cm2
= ({tex}\frac {36}{6}{/tex}){tex}\pi{/tex} cm2
= 6 {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\frac {22}{7}{/tex}) cm2
= {tex}\frac {132}{7}{/tex} cm2

questions.question: In a circle of radius 21 cm, an arc subtends an angle of 60o at the centre. The area of the sector formed by the arc is:
questions.explanation:
The angle subtended by the arc = 60o
So, area of the sector = ({tex}\frac {60^o}{360^o}{/tex}) {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex}r2 cm2
= ({tex}\frac {441}{6}{/tex}) {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\frac {22}{7}{/tex})cm2
= 231 cm2

questions.question: Find the area of the sector if the radius is 5 cm and with an angle of 50o.
questions.explanation:
The area of the sector {tex}=\frac{x^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{50^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 5^2{/tex}
= 10.90 cm

questions.question: If the area of a sector POK is 100{tex}\pi{/tex} sq.units and angle POK = 49o, then find the radius of sector
questions.explanation:
Area of sector {tex}=\frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{49}{360} \times \pi r^2=100 \pi{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{49}{360} \times r^2=100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow r^2=\frac{100 \times 360}{49}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow r=\frac{10 \times 6 \sqrt{10}}{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow r=\frac{60 \sqrt{10}}{7}{/tex} units

questions.question: O is the centre of a circle of diameter 4 cm and OABC is a square, if the shaded area is {tex}\frac 13{/tex} area of the square, then the side of the square is _______.
questions.explanation:
Let the length of side of square be x cm
Then area of square = x2 cm2
Area of sector of circle
= {tex}\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{90^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} angle of square = {tex}\theta{/tex} = 90o]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Shaded area = {tex}\frac{\pi \times 4}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\pi{/tex}
According to question, Area of square = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} shaded area
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3{tex}\pi{/tex} = x2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = {tex}\sqrt {3\pi}{/tex} cm

questions.question: A chord of a circle subtends an angle of 60o at the centre. If the length of the chord is 100 cm, find the area of the major segment.
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle {/tex}OAB, by angle sum property

{tex}60^{\circ}+\angle O A B+\angle O B A=180^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \angle O A B=120^{\circ} \Rightarrow \angle O A B=60^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \triangle {/tex}OAB is an equilateral triangle.
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} r = 100 cm
Area of major segment
= Area of major sector + Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}OAB
= {tex}\frac{\left(360^{\circ}-60^{\circ}\right)}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2+\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} r^2{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{300}{360} \times \frac{22}{7} \times(100)^2+\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 100^2{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{5}{6} \times \frac{22}{7} \times(100)^2+\sqrt{3} \times 2500{/tex}
= 26190.48 + 4330.13 = 30520.61 cm2

questions.question: In the figure, ABDCA represents a quadrant of a circle of radius 7 cm a with centre A. Find the area of the shaded portion.
questions.explanation:
Area of quadrant = {tex}\frac 14{/tex}{tex}\pi r^2{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{22}{7} \times(7)^2=\frac{77}{2}{/tex} cm2 = 38.5 cm2
Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}BAE = {tex}\frac 12 \times{/tex} base {tex}\times{/tex} height
= {tex}\frac 12{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} AB {tex}\times{/tex} AE = {tex}\frac 12{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 7 cm2
Hence, area of the shaded portion = Area of the quadrant ABDCA - Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}BAE
= (38.5 - 7) cm2 = 31.5 cm2

questions.question: If the perimeter of a sector of a circle of radius 5.2 cm is 16.4 cm, then the area of the sector is ________.
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of a sector of circle = {tex}\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times 2 \pi r{/tex} + 2r
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times 2 \pi \times 5.2\right){/tex} + (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5.2) = 16.4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \pi=\frac{16.4-10.4}{10.4}=\frac{6}{10.4}{/tex}
Area of sector of circle = {tex}\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{6}{10.4} \times(5.2)^2{/tex} = 15.6 cm2

questions.question: In the given figure (not drawn to scale), AP = AQ = 3 cm, the area of the shaded region is ________.
questions.explanation:
In quadrilateral ABCD
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 360o
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + 90o + 90o + 60o = 360o {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 120o
Now, area of shaded region = {tex}\frac{120^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi \times 3^2{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times 3 \times 3=3 \pi{/tex} cm2

questions.question: A car has two wipers which do not overlap. Each wiper has a blade of length 42 cm sweeping through an angle of 120o. Find the total area cleaned at each sweep of the blades.
questions.explanation:
Clearly, each wiper sweeps a sector of a circle of radius 42 cm and sector angle 120o.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total area cleaned at each sweep = 2 {tex}\times \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
= 2 {tex}\times \frac{120^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 42 \times{/tex} 42 cm2 = 3696 cm2

questions.question: Pankaj has a motorcycle with wheels of diameter 91 cm. There are 22 spokes in the wheel. Find the length of arc between two adjoining spokes.
questions.explanation:
Radius of wheel = {tex}\frac {91} 2{/tex}cm
Angle between two adjoining spokes, {tex}\theta=\frac{360^{\circ}}{22}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Length of arc = {tex}\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times 2 \pi r{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{360^{\circ} \times 22} \times 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times \frac{91}{2}{/tex} = 13 cm

questions.question:
Match the shaded region given in Column-I with their areas in Column-ll.

Column-I Column-II
(a)  (i) 262.50 cm2
(b)  (ii) 271.04 cm2
(c)  (iii) 427.14 cm2
questions.explanation: (a) - (ii), (b) - (iii), (c) - (i)

questions.question: The area of a sector of angle {tex}\alpha{/tex} (in degrees) of a circle with radius R is:
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{\alpha}{360} \times \pi \mathrm{R}^2{/tex}

questions.question: The area of the sector of a circle of radius 10.5 cm is 69.3 cm2. Find the central angle of the sector.
questions.explanation:
It is given that area of the sector = 69.3 cm2
and Radius = 10.5 cm
Now, Area of the sector {tex}= \frac { \pi r^ { 2 } \theta } { 360 }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { \pi \times ( 10.5 ) ^ { 2 } \times \theta } { 360 }{/tex} = 69.3
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta = \frac { 69.3 \times 360 \times 7 } { 10.5 \times 10.5 \times 22 }{/tex} = 72o
Therefore, Central angle of the sector = 72o

questions.question: Find the area of a sector of angle p (in degrees) of a circle with radius R.
questions.explanation:
Area of sector = {tex}\frac { \theta } { 360 } \times \pi r ^ { 2 }{/tex}
where {tex}\theta{/tex} = angle, r = radius of circle
Here, we have
{tex}\theta {/tex} = p and radius = R
Putting these in formula,
Area of sector {tex}= \frac { p } { 360 } \times \pi R ^ { 2 }{/tex}

questions.question: A sector of a circle of radius 8 cm contains an angle of 135. Find the area of the sector.
questions.explanation:
It is given that the radius of circle = 8 cm
and angle, {tex}\theta {/tex} = 135o
Therefore, area of sector {tex} = \frac{\theta }{{360^\circ }} \times \pi {r^2}{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{135^\circ }}{{360^\circ }} \times \pi \times 8 \times 8{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{135^\circ }}{{360^\circ }} \times \pi \times 64{/tex}
{tex} = 24\pi {/tex} cm2

questions.question: Find the area of the sector of a circle having radius 6 cm and of angle 30o.
questions.explanation:
Radius of a circle = r = 6 cm
Central angle {tex}= \theta {/tex} = 30o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of the sector {tex}= \frac { \pi r ^ { 2 } \theta } { 360 }{/tex}
{tex}= \left( \frac { 3.14 \times 6 \times 6 \times 30 ^ { \circ } } { 360 ^ { \circ } } \right) {/tex} cm2
= 9.42 cm2

questions.question: Find the area of a sector of a circle of radius 28 cm and central angle 45o.
questions.explanation:
We know that the area A of a sector of a circle of radius r and central angle {tex}\theta{/tex}(in degrees) is given by
{tex}A=\frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi r^{2}{/tex}
Here, r = 28 cm and {tex}\theta{/tex} = 45.
{tex}\therefore{/tex}{tex}\begin{aligned} &A=\frac{45}{360} \times \pi \times(28)^{2} \end{aligned}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{8} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 28 \times 28 \mathrm{cm}^{2}{/tex} = 308 cm2

questions.question: What is the formula to calculate the area of a sector?
questions.explanation:
The area of the sector is {tex}\frac{x^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
Where x° is the degree measure of the angle at the center and r is the radius of the circle.

questions.question: Find the area of the sector if the radius is 12 cm and with an angle of 134o.
questions.explanation:
The area of the sector {tex}=\frac{x^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{134^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 12^2{/tex}
= 168.38 cm2

questions.question: A man goes to a walking track twice a day in the shape of a sector with an angle of 123o and a radius of 138 m. Find the area covered by the man of the walking track in a day.
questions.explanation:
The area of the sector {tex}=\frac{x^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{123^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 138^2{/tex}
= 20441.4 m2
Area covered by the man of the walking track in a day = 20441.4 + 20441.4
= 40882.8 m2

questions.question: A horse is grazing in a field. It is tied to a pole with a rope of length 6 m. The horse moves from point A to point B making an arch with an angle of 70°. Find the area of the sector grazed by the horse.
questions.explanation:
The area of the sector {tex}=\frac{x^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{70^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 6^2{/tex}
21.99 m

questions.question: Find the area of the segment if the area of the sector is 44 m and the part of a triangle in the sector is 12 m.
questions.explanation:
The area of the segment {tex}=\left(\frac{x^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2\right)-\frac{b h}{2}{/tex}
= Area of the sector - Area of the triangle
= 44 - 12
= 32 m2

questions.question: PQ is a chord of a circle with centre O and radius 6 cm. PQ is of length 6 cm and divides the circle into two segments. The area of the minor segment is
questions.explanation:
Area of the minor segment = Area of sector OPCQ - area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}OPQ
Area of the minor segment {tex}=\left\{\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times r^2\right\} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\left\{\frac{60^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi \times(6)^2-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}(6)^2\right\}{/tex} ..({tex}\theta{/tex} = 60o, r = 6 cm)
{tex}=\left\{\frac{1}{6} \times \pi \times 36-\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 36\right\}=(6 \pi-9 \sqrt{3}) \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
Hence, the area of minor segment = {tex}(6 \pi-9 \sqrt{3}) \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: The length of the minute hand of a wall clock is 7 cm, then area swept by the minute hand in 10 minutes is
questions.explanation:
In 1 minute, minute hand makes an angle of 6o
so, in 10 minutes, minute hand will make an angle of 60o
Area swept by minute hand in 10 minutes = Area of sector of radius 7 cm with angle 60o
Area of sector {tex}=\frac{x^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi \times r^2{/tex}
So, Area swept by minute hand in 10 minutes = {tex}\frac{60^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7^2{/tex}
= 25.66 cm2

questions.question: The length of a minute hand of a wall clock is 7 cm. What is the area swept by it in 30 minutes is
questions.explanation:
For a minute hand, 60 minutes is equivalent to 360o and so 30 minutes will be 180o.
Area swept in 60 minutes is area of full circle.
So area swept in 30 minutes will be area of half circle.
Thus, area swept {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times\left(\frac{22}{7}\right) \times 7^2{/tex} = 77 cm2

questions.question: In the given figure PQ and RS are the perpendicular diameters of the circle whose centre is O and radius = 14 cm. the area of the shaded region is
questions.explanation:
For Triangle POS,
PO = OS = 14cm
Now Get PS by pythagoras theorem.
Again, Required area = {tex}\frac14{/tex}Area of Circle - Area POS

questions.question: A pendulum swings through an angle of 30o and describes an arc 8.8 cm in length. Find the length of the pendulum.
questions.explanation:
Length of the pendulum = Radius of a sector of the circle
Arc length = 8.8
{tex}\frac{θ}{360} (2{tex}\pi{/tex}r){/tex} = 8.8
{tex}\frac{30}{360} \times 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times r{/tex} = 8.8
r = 16.8 cm

questions.question: Three horses are tethered with 7-meter-long ropes at the three corners of a triangular field having sides 20 m, 34 m, and 42 m. The area of the plot which can be grazed by the horses is
questions.explanation: 77 m2

questions.question: Area of a quadrant of circle whose circumference is 22 cm is ({tex}\pi=\frac{22}{7}{/tex})
questions.explanation:
We have
C = 22 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \pi \mathrm{r}{/tex} = 22 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times r{/tex} = 22
{tex}\therefore{/tex} r = 3.5 cm
Now,
Area of quadrant of a circle {tex}=\frac{\pi r^2}{4}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{22}{7} \times \frac{(3.5)^2}{4}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{22 \times 12.25}{28}{/tex}
= 9.625 cm2

questions.question: The length of an arc that subtends an angle of 24o at the centre of a circle with 5 cm radius is
questions.explanation:
Given that, {tex}\theta{/tex} = 30o and r = 4 cm
Length of an arc {tex}=\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}(2 \pi r){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{30^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times 2 \pi \times 4{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2 \pi \times 4}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Length of an arc {tex}=\frac{2 \pi}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: The angle of the major sector and the corresponding minor sector of a circle are
questions.explanation: Conjugate Angles

questions.question: The area of a sector of a circle of radius 5 cm is 5{tex}\pi{/tex} cm2. The angle contained by the sector will be
questions.explanation:
Area of the sector {tex}=\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
{tex}5 \pi=\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi \times 5 \times 5{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta =\frac{5 \pi}{\pi} \times \frac{360^{\circ}}{5 \times 5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta{/tex} = 72o
Hence, sector angle = 72o

questions.question: To warn ships of underwater rocks, a light house spreads a red colored light over a sector of angle 80o to a distance of 16.5 km. Find the area of the sea over which the ships are warned. (Use {tex}\pi{/tex} = 3.14)
questions.explanation:
When a lighthouse spreads a red-colored light underwater, it forms a sector of angle 80o and radius = 16.5km
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of sector {tex}=\frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi \times r^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of sector {tex}=\frac{80}{360} \times 3.14 \times 16.5 \times 16.5{/tex}
= 189.97 km2

questions.question: The minute hand of a clock is 10 cm long. Find the area of the face of the clock described by the minute hand between 8 am and 8.25 am.
questions.explanation:
Here the angle swept is 150o. We need to find the area of this sector which subtends 150o at the centre.
So, area {tex}=\pi r^2 \times \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{22}{7} \times 10^2 \times \frac{150}{360}{/tex}
= 130.95 cm2

questions.question: The perimeter of a certain sector of a circle of radius 6.5 cm is 31 cm. The area of the sector will be
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of a sector of circle = 31 cm
Radius = 6.5 cm
Arc length = 31 - (6.5 + 6.5) = 18 cm
Now, Area of sector {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times{/tex} Arc length {tex}\times{/tex} radius {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times 18 \times 6.5{/tex} = 58.5 cm2

questions.question: A circular disc of radius 6 cm is divided into three sectors with central angles 90o, 120o and 150o. The ratio of the areas of the three sectors is
questions.explanation:
Area of sector Having central angle 90 degree : Area of sector Having central angle 120 degree : Area of sector Having central angle 150 degree
{tex}= \frac{90}{360} \times \pi \times 6^2 : \frac{120}{360} \times \pi \times 6^2 : \frac{150}{360} \times \pi \times 6^2 {/tex}
{tex}= \frac14 :\frac13 : \frac5{12}{/tex}
= 3 : 4 : 5

questions.question: If the area of a sector of circle of radius 36 cm is {tex} 54 \pi {~cm}^2 {/tex}, then the length of the corresponding arc of the sector is:
questions.explanation:
We know that:
Area of a sector {tex}=\frac{\pi r^2 \theta}{360^{\circ}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\pi \times 36 \times 36 \times \theta}{360}=54{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta=\frac{54 \times 360}{36 \times 36}=15^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Length of arc {tex}=\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times 2 \pi r{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{15}{360} \times 2 \pi \times 36{/tex}
= 3{tex}\pi{/tex} cm

questions.question: The difference between the semi-perimeter and the sides of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC are 7 cm, 5 cm and 3 cm respectively. The perimeter of the triangle is ________.
questions.explanation:
Let the sides of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC be a, b, c
Then, (s - a) = 7 cm, (s - b) = 5 cm and (s - c) = 3 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (s - a) + (s - b) + (s - c) = 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3s - (a + b + c) = 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3s - 2s = 15 {tex}\left[\because s=\frac{a+b+c}{2}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} s = 15 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Perimeter = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 30 cm

questions.question: The perimeter of a triangle is 300 m and its sides are in the ratio 3 : 5 : 7. Find its area.
questions.explanation:
Let the sides of the triangle be
a = 3x, b = 5x, c = 7x
Perimeter of triangle = 300 m
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 5x + 7x = 300 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15x = 300 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 20
{tex}\therefore{/tex} a = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 60 m, b = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 100 m,
c = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 140 m
Now, {tex}s=\frac{300}{2}=150 \mathrm{~m}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of triangle {tex}\sqrt{150(150-60)(150-100)(150-140)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{150 \times 90 \times 50 \times 10} \mathrm{~m}^2=1500 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~m}^2 .{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, the area of the {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is
questions.explanation:
Here a = 11 cm, b = 4 cm, c = 8 cm
{tex}\therefore s=\frac{11+4+8}{2}=\frac{23}{2}=11.5 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
Area
{tex}=\sqrt{11.5 \times(11.5-11) \times(11.5-4) \times(11.5-8)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{11.5 \times 0.5 \times 7.5 \times 3.5}=\sqrt{150.94}{/tex} = 12.29 cm2

questions.question: A triangle and a parallelogram have the same base and the same area. If the sides of the triangle are 26 cm, 28 cm and 30 cm and the parallelogram stands on the base 28 cm, then find the height of the parallelogram.
questions.explanation:
For the given triangle, we have
a = 28 cm, b = 30 cm, c = 26 cm

So, {tex}s=\frac{a+b+c}{2}=\frac{28+30+26}{2}=\frac{84}{2}{/tex} = 42 cm
Area of the triangle {tex}\sqrt{42(42-28)(42-30)(42-26)} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{42 \times 14 \times 12 \times 16} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{112896} \mathrm{~cm}^2=336 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
As, Area of the parallelogram = Area of the triangle
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of the parallelogram = 336 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base x height = 336 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 28 x h = 336
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}h=\frac{336}{28}=12 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
Thus, the height of the parallelogram = 12 cm

questions.question: In the given parallelogram, find the length of the altitude from vertex A on the side DC.
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD, let a = 12cm, b = 17cm and c = 25 cm.
{tex}\therefore s=\left(\frac{12+17+25}{2}\right) \mathrm{cm}=\frac{54}{2} \mathrm{~cm}{/tex} = 27 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD {tex}=\sqrt{27(27-12)(27-17)(27-25)} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{27 \times 15 \times 10 \times 2} \mathrm{~cm}^2=90 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Now, area of parallelogram ABCD
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD
= (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 90) cm2 = 180 cm2 ...(i)
Let length of altitude of parallelogram ABCD from vertex A be h cm.
Also, area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Altitude
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 180 = DC {tex}\times{/tex} h [From (i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 180 = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} h
{tex}\Rightarrow h=\frac{180}{12}{/tex} = 15 cm
Required length of the altitude is 15 cm.

questions.question: A rhombus shaped sheet with perimeter 40 cm and one diagonal 12 cm, is painted on both sides at the rate of ₹ 5 per cm2. Find the cost of painting.
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be a rhombus having sides AB = BC = CD = DA = x cm
Perimeter of rhombus = 40 cm [Given]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + x + x + x = 40
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 40
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 10
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, let a = 10 cm, b = 12 cm and c = 10 cm
Now, semi-perimeter of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, s {tex}=\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{10+10+12}{2}\right) \mathrm{cm}=\frac{32}{2} \mathrm{~cm}=16 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC {tex}=\sqrt{16(16-10)(16-12)(16-10)} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{16 \times 6 \times 4 \times 6} \mathrm{~cm}^2=48 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Now, area of the rhombus ABCD
= 2(Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC) = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 48) cm2 = 96 cm2
Cost of painting the sheet of area 1 cm2 = ₹5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost of painting the sheet of area 96 cm2 = f (96 {tex}\times{/tex} 5) = ₹ 480
Thus, the cost of painting the sheet on both sides
= ₹(2 {tex}\times{/tex} 480) = ₹ 960

questions.question: The area of a triangle, two sides of which are 8 cm and 11 cm and the perimeter is 32 cm is {tex}k \sqrt{30} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}. Find the value of k.
questions.explanation:
Here we have perimeter of the triangle = 32 cm
Let a = 8 cm and b = 11 cm
Third side, c = 32 - (8 + 11) = 13 cm
{tex}\therefore s=\frac{32}{2}=16 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
Therefore, area of the triangle
{tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}=\sqrt{16 \times 8 \times 5 \times 3} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=8 \sqrt{30} \mathrm{~cm}^2=k \sqrt{30} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex} (given)
{tex}\therefore k \sqrt{30}=8 \sqrt{30} \Rightarrow k=8{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC has sides AB = 7.5 cm, AC = 6.5 cm and BC = 7 cm. On base BC a parallelogram DBCE of same area as that of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is constructed. Find the height DF of the parallelogram.
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, a = 7 cm, b = 6.5 cm and c = 7.5 cm
{tex}\therefore s=\left(\frac{7.5+7+6.5}{2}\right) \mathrm{cm}=\frac{21}{2} \mathrm{~cm}{/tex} = 10.5 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC {tex}=\sqrt{10.5(10.5-7.5)(10.5-7)(10.5-6.5)} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{10.5 \times 3 \times 3.5 \times 4} \mathrm{~cm}^2=\sqrt{441} \mathrm{~cm}^2=21 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Since, Area of AABC = Area of parallelogram BCED
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 21 = BC {tex}\times{/tex} DF {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 21 = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} DF
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}D F=\frac{21}{7}{/tex} = 3 cm

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 11 cm, 15 cm and 16 cm. The altitude to the largest side is ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, sides of triangle 11 cm, 15 cm and 16 cm.
{tex}\therefore s=\frac{11+15+16}{2}=21 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of triangle {tex}=\sqrt{21(21-11)(21-15)(21-16)}{/tex}
{tex}=30 \sqrt{7} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Let altitude to the largest side be h cm
{tex}\therefore \frac{1}{2} \times 16 \times h=30 \sqrt{7} \Rightarrow 8 h=30 \sqrt{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow h=\frac{15 \sqrt{7}}{4} \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}

questions.question: A field is in the shape of a trapezium whose parallel sides are 77 cm and 60 cm. The non-parallel sides are 25 cm and 26 cm. Find the area of the field.
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be the trapezium with sides AB = 77 cm, CD = 60 cm, AD = 25 cm, BC = 26 cm.

We draw CE || AD
{tex} \therefore{/tex} Area of trapezium ABCD
= area of parallelogram {tex}\triangle{/tex}ECD + area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ECB
Now, In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ECB
{tex}s=\frac{25+17+26}{2}=34{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}EBC {tex}=\sqrt{34(34-25)(34-17)(34-26)}=204 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Also, Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}EBC = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times B E \times h \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \times 17 \times h=204{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} h = 24 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of parallelogram AECD = AE {tex}\times{/tex} h = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 = 1440 cm2
Hence, area of trapezium ABCD
= (1440 + 204) cm2 = 1644 cm2

questions.question: If the sides of a triangular field measure 51 m, 37 m and 20 m, then find the cost of levelling it at ₹ 7 per m2.
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}s=\frac{1}{2}(51+37+20) \mathrm{m}=54 \mathrm{~m}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area {tex}=\sqrt{54(54-51)(54-37)(54-20)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{54 \times 3 \times 17 \times 34} \mathrm{~m}^2=306 \mathrm{~m}^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost of levelling the field = ₹(306 {tex}\times{/tex} 7) = ₹ 2142

questions.question: The base of an isosceles triangle measures 24 cm and its area is 192 cm2. Find its perimeter.
questions.explanation:
Let the each of the other two equal sides of an isosceles triangle be a cm.
Then, {tex}s=\frac{a+a+24}{2}=(a+12) \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
Area of triangle = 192 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{(a+12)(a+12-a)(a+12-a)(a+12-24)}{/tex} = 192
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 144 (a2 - 144) = (192)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 = 400 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 20 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Perimeter = 20 + 20 + 24 = 64 cm

questions.question: A conical tent is made by stitching 12 triangular pieces of cloth of two different colours as shown in the given figure. Each piece measuring 11 m, 11 m and 6 m. How much cloth of each colour is required for the conical tent?
questions.explanation:
We have, a = 11 m, 5 = 11 m and c = 6 m
{tex}s=\frac{11+11+6}{2}{/tex} = 14 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of one triangular piece
{tex}=\sqrt{14 \times 3 \times 3 \times 8}=31.75 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of 6 identical triangular pieces = 31.75 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 190.5 cm2
Similarly, area of another 6 triangular pieces = 190.5 cm2

questions.question: The perimeter of a field in the form of an equilateral triangle is 36 cm, then its area is given by
questions.explanation:
Since all the sides are equal in an equilateral triangle.
So, perimeter = a + a + a, where a is the side of equilateral triangle.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3a = 36 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 12 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of equilateral triangle {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} a^2=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}(12)^2=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 144{/tex}
{tex}=36 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: Tanya joined four triangles of cardboard to create a mask of Joker as shown in the given figure. Find the total area of the mask. {tex}\text { [Given } \sqrt{2}=1.41, \sqrt{3}=1.73 \text { ] }{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Total area of I & II part
{tex}=2 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times(2)^2=2 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}^2=3.46 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
In fig.(lll), {tex}s=\frac{6+11+15}{2}{/tex} = 16 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total area of III & IV part {tex}2 \times \sqrt{16 \times 10 \times 5 \times 1}=56.56 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
Hence, total area of the mask = (3.46 + 56.56) cm2 = 60.02 cm2

questions.question: Suman made a picture with some white paper and a single coloured paper as shown in figure. White paper is available at her home and free of cost. The cost of coloured paper used is at the rate of 10p per cm2. Find the total cost of the coloured paper used. (Take {tex}\sqrt3{/tex} = 1.732 and {tex}\sqrt{11}{/tex} = 3.32)
questions.explanation:
Area of I {tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times(10)^2=25 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}^2=43.3 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
(II) {tex}s=\frac{10+9+3}{2}=11 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of II {tex}=2 \sqrt{11 \times 1 \times 2 \times 8}=8 \sqrt{11} \mathrm{~cm}^2=26.56 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
(IV) {tex}s=\frac{20+20+4}{2}=22 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of IV {tex}=2 \sqrt{22 \times 2 \times 2 \times 18}{/tex}
{tex}=24 \sqrt{11} \mathrm{~cm}^2=79.68 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total area of coloured paper used
= (43.3 + 26.56 + 79.68) cm2 = 149.54 cm2
Cost of coloured paper used = {tex}\frac{10}{100} \times 149.54=₹ 14.95{/tex}

questions.question: A design is made on a rectangular tile of dimensions 50 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 70 cm as shown in figure. The design shows 8 triangles, each of sides 26 cm, 17 cm and 25 cm. Find the total area of the design and the remaining area of the tile respectively.
questions.explanation:
Area of rectangular tile = (50 {tex}\times{/tex} 70) cm2 = 3500 cm2
We have,
a = 25 cm, b = 17 cm and c = 26 cm
{tex}\therefore s=\frac{a+b+c}{2}=\left(\frac{25+17+26}{2}\right) \mathrm{cm}=34 \mathrm{~cm}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of 1 triangle {tex}=\sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{34(34-25)(34-17)(34-26)} \mathrm{cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{34 \times 9 \times 17 \times 8} \mathrm{~cm}^2=204 \mathrm{~cm}^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total area of 8 triangles = (204 {tex}\times{/tex} 8) cm2 = 1632 cm2
So, area of the design = 1632 cm2
Also, remaining area of the tile = (3500 - 1632) cm2 = 1868 cm2

questions.question: Find the area of quadrilateral ABCD in which AB = 9 cm, BC = 40 cm, CD = 28 cm, DA = 15 cm and {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 90°.
questions.explanation:
In right angled {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC, using Pythagoras theorem (AC)2 = (9)2 + (40)2 = 1681
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AC = 41 cm

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADC {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \times 9 \times 40{/tex} = 180 cm2
Hence, area of quadrilateral ABCD
= (180 + 126) cm2 = 306 cm2

questions.question: Area of a segment of a circle of radius r and central angle 90o is:
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{\pi r^2}{4}-\frac{1}{2} r^2{/tex}

questions.question: The area of a quadrant of a circle whose circumference is 616 cm will be
questions.explanation:
{tex}2\pi \text R{/tex} = 616
R = {tex}\frac {(616 \times 7)}{(2 \times 22)}{/tex}
R = 98 cm
Area of quadrant = {tex}\frac {\pi \text r^2}{4}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac {(22 \times 98 \times 98)}{(7\times 4)}{/tex}
= 7546 cm2

questions.question: Area of a sector of angle {tex}\theta{/tex} (in degrees) of a circle with radius r is:
questions.explanation:
Area of circle = {tex}\pi r^2{/tex}
And
Fraction of a sector of angle {tex}\theta{/tex} in a circle
{tex}=\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of the sector
{tex}=\frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi r^2 {/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi r^2\right) \times \frac{2}{2}=\frac{\theta}{720} \times 2 \pi r^2{/tex}

questions.question: The difference of the areas of a minor sector of angle 120o and its corresponding major sector of a circle of radius 21 cm, is
questions.explanation:
ar. major sector {tex}\frac{240^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi \times(21)^2{/tex}
{tex} =\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times 21 {/tex}
{tex} =44 \times 21{/tex}
= 924 cm2
ar. minor sector
{tex}=\frac{120^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times 21{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times 21{/tex}
= 462 cm2
difference between area = 924 - 462
= 462 cm2

questions.question: The area of the sector of a circle of radius 12 cm is {tex}60\pi{/tex} cm2. The central angle of this sector is:
questions.explanation:
area of sector = 60 {tex}\pi{/tex} cm2
Let centre angle = {tex}\theta{/tex}
area {tex}=\frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi r^2{/tex}
{tex}\frac {60 \times 360^{\circ}}{12 \times 12} = \theta{/tex}
= 150o = {tex}\theta{/tex}
Central angle = 150o

questions.question: If the area of a sector of a circle is {tex}\frac{1}{8}{/tex} of the area of the circle, then the central angle of the sector is:
questions.explanation:
Given
{tex} \frac{\text { area of sector }}{\text { area of circle }}=\frac{1}{8} {/tex}
{tex} \frac{\frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times \pi r^2}{\pi r^2}=\frac{1}{8} {/tex}
{tex} \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}}=\frac{1}{8} {/tex}
{tex} \theta=\frac{360^{\circ}}{8} {/tex}
{tex} \theta=45^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: The area of a sector of a circle of radius 16 cm cut off by an arc, which is 18.5 cm long, is:
questions.explanation: {tex} Area \ of \ the \ sector=\dfrac{Arc \ length}{2\pi r }\times \pi r^2\\ =\dfrac{18.5 }{ 2π(16) } \times [ π 16² ] \\ \bf = 148 \ cm²{/tex}

questions.question: The ratio of the area of a quadrant of a circle to the area of the same circle is:
questions.explanation: {tex}1: 4{/tex}

questions.question: The perimeter of the shaded region in the given figure is:
questions.explanation: {tex}l+2 {r}{/tex}

questions.question: An arc of length 22 cm subtends an angle of {tex}x^{\circ}{/tex} at the centre of the circle. If radius of circle is 36 cm, the value of {tex}x{/tex} is
The length of arc subtending an angle of {tex}210^{\circ}{/tex} at the centre of the circle, is {tex}\frac{44}{3} {~cm}{/tex}. The radius of the circle is:
questions.explanation: 4 cm

questions.question: An arc of length '{tex}l{/tex}' subtends an angle of {tex}15^{\circ}{/tex} at the centre of a circle of radius 8.4 cm. The value of {tex}l{/tex} is
questions.explanation: {tex}2.2 {~cm}{/tex}

questions.question: OAB is sector of a circle with centre O and radius 7 cm. If length of arc {tex}\overparen{{AB}}=\frac{22}{3} {~cm}{/tex}, then {tex}\angle {AOB}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: {tex}60^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: The angle of the sector of a circle whose area is one-eighth of the area of the circle is:
questions.explanation: {tex}45^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: A piece of wire 20 cm long is bent into the form of an arc of a circle of radius {tex}\frac{60}{\pi} {~cm}{/tex}. The angle subtended by the arc at the centre of the circle is:
questions.explanation: {tex}60^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, the shaded region represents:
questions.explanation: minor segment

questions.question: If a sector of a circle has an area of {tex}40 \pi{/tex} sq. units and a central angle of {tex}72^{\circ}{/tex}, the radius of the circle is:
questions.explanation: {tex}10 \sqrt{2}{/tex} units

questions.question: The degree measure of the angle at the centre of a quadrant of a circle is:
questions.explanation: {tex}90^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: The area of a quadrant of a circle of radius '{tex}2 r{/tex}' is:
questions.explanation: {tex}\pi {r}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: OAB is sector of a circle with centre O and radius 7 cm . If length of arc {tex}\overparen{{AB}}=\frac{22}{3} {~cm}{/tex}, then {tex}\angle {AOB}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: {tex}60^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: The area of a sector of a circle with radius 7 cm, when the angle of the sector is {tex}60^{\circ}{/tex}, is:
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{77}{3}{/tex} sq. cm.

questions.question: If a large circular pizza is divided into 5 equal sectors, then the central angle of each sector will be:
questions.explanation: {tex}72^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: An arc of a circle is of length {tex}5 \pi {~cm}{/tex} and the sector it bounds has an area of {tex}20 \pi {~cm}^{2}{/tex}. Its radius is:
questions.explanation: 8 cm

questions.question: If an arc of a circle of diameter 10 cm subtends an angle of {tex}144^{\circ}{/tex} at the centre of the circle, then the length of the arc is:
questions.explanation: {tex}4 \pi {~cm}{/tex}

questions.question: {tex}A{/tex} and {tex}B{/tex} are sectors of two different circles. Radius of sector {tex}A{/tex} is double of that of sector {tex}B{/tex} whereas central angle of sector {tex}B{/tex} is double the central angle of sector A. The ratio of the area of sector A to the area of sector B is:
questions.explanation: {tex}2: 1{/tex}

questions.question: If length of an arc of a circle subtending an angle {tex}\theta{/tex} at the centre is numerically equal to the area of the sector formed by it, then the radius of the circle is:
questions.explanation: 2 units

questions.question: When degree measure of an angle subtended by an arc at the centre of a circle is {tex}90^{\circ}{/tex}, the area of the corresponding sector of the circle of radius r, is:
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{1}{4} \pi r^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If the sector of a circle with diameter 14 cm makes an angle {tex}90^{\circ}{/tex} at the centre, then the perimeter of the sector is:
If the length of an arc of a circle of diameter 84 cm is 88 cm, then the angle subtended by the arc at the centre of the circle is:
questions.explanation: {tex}120^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: If the length of an arc of a circle subtending an angle {tex}60^{\circ}{/tex} at its centre is 22 cm, then the radius of the circle is:
The diagonals of a rhombus ABCD intersect at O. Taking 'O' as the centre, an arc of radius 6 cm is drawn intersecting OA and OD at E and F respectively. The area of the sector OEF is:
questions.explanation: {tex}9 \pi {~cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: Using Baudhāyana's method, a HPQS rectangle with sides 24 meters and 6 meters is to be 'squared'. What is the side length of the square that is finally constructed?
questions.explanation:
The Baudhāyana method constructs a square (HPQS) whose area is equal to the area of the original rectangle.

Area of the rectangle = length × width = 24 m × 6 m = 144 m².

If 's' is the side length of the constructed square, then s² = 144 m².

s = {tex}\sqrt {144}{/tex}= 12 meters.

The side length of the square HPQS is HP, so HP = 12 meters.

questions.question: A satellite orbits the Earth in a circular path with a radius of 4200 km. If it completes 1/8th of its orbit, what distance has it traveled?
questions.explanation:
The circumference of a circular orbit is {tex}2\pi r{/tex}. The satellite travels 1/8th of its orbit. 

Radius ({tex}r{/tex}) = 4200 km.

Distance traveled = {tex}\frac{1}{8} \times 2\pi r = \frac{1}{4} \pi r{/tex}.

Substituting the value of {tex}r{/tex}: Distance traveled = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \times \pi \times 4200 = 1050\pi{/tex} km.

questions.question: If the radius of a circular table is 35 cm, what is the perimeter of a circular table? (Use π = 22/7)
questions.explanation:
The perimeter of a circular table is its circumference (C).

The formula for circumference is C = 2πr, where r is the radius.

Given:

{tex}r = 35 \text{ cm}{/tex}

{tex}\pi = \frac{22}{7}{/tex}

Substituting the values into the formula:

{tex}C = 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 35{/tex}

{tex}C = 2 \times 22 \times 5{/tex}

{tex}C = 44 \times 5{/tex}

{tex}C = 220 \text{ cm}{/tex}

questions.question: A hexagonal park is designed inside a circular boundary. If the radius of the circle is 10 m and the hexagon is regular and inscribed, what is the area of the hexagon? (Use {tex}\sqrt{3} = 1.732{/tex})
questions.explanation:
Given:

Radius of the circle {tex}r = 10{/tex} m

The hexagon is regular and inscribed in the circle.

{tex}\sqrt{3} = 1.732{/tex}

For a regular hexagon inscribed in a circle, the side length {tex}s{/tex} of the hexagon is equal to the radius {tex}r{/tex} of the circle.

So, {tex}s = 10{/tex} m.

A regular hexagon can be divided into 6 equilateral triangles, each with side length {tex}s{/tex}.

Area of one equilateral triangle {tex}A_{triangle} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} s^2{/tex}

Area of the hexagon {tex}A_{hexagon} = 6 \times A_{triangle}{/tex}

{tex}A_{hexagon} = 6 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} s^2{/tex}

{tex}A_{hexagon} = \frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} s^2{/tex}

Substituting the values:

{tex}A_{hexagon} = \frac{3 \times 1.732}{2} \times (10)^2{/tex}

{tex}A_{hexagon} = \frac{5.196}{2} \times 100{/tex}

{tex}A_{hexagon} = 2.598 \times 100{/tex}

{tex}A_{hexagon} = 259.8{/tex} {tex}\text{m}^2{/tex}

questions.question: A square is inscribed in a circle with a radius of 14 cm. What is the area of the square? (Use {tex}\pi = 22/7{/tex})
questions.explanation:
Given:

Radius of the circle {tex}r = 14{/tex} cm

For a square inscribed in a circle, the diagonal of the square is equal to the diameter of the circle.

Diameter {tex}d = 2r = 2 \times 14 = 28{/tex} cm.

Let the side of the square be {tex}s{/tex}.

By Pythagorean theorem for the square, {tex}s^2 + s^2 = d^2{/tex}

{tex}2s^2 = d^2{/tex}

{tex}2s^2 = (28)^2{/tex}

{tex}2s^2 = 784{/tex}

{tex}s^2 = \frac{784}{2}{/tex}

{tex}s^2 = 392{/tex}

The area of the square is {tex}s^2{/tex}.

{tex}A_{square} = 392{/tex} {tex}\text{cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: A circular pizza has a radius of 21 cm. If a slice (sector) of pizza is cut with an angle of 45° at the center, what is the area of this slice? (Use {tex}\pi = 22/7{/tex})
questions.explanation:
Given:

Radius {tex}r = 21{/tex} cm

Angle of the sector {tex}\theta = 45^\circ{/tex}

{tex}\pi = 22/7{/tex}

Formula for the area of a sector:

{tex}A = \frac{\theta}{360^\circ} \times \pi r^2{/tex}

Substituting the values:

{tex}A = \frac{45}{360} \times \frac{22}{7} \times (21)^2{/tex}

{tex}A = \frac{1}{8} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 441{/tex}

{tex}A = \frac{1}{8} \times 22 \times \frac{441}{7}{/tex}

{tex}A = \frac{1}{8} \times 22 \times 63{/tex}

{tex}A = \frac{1386}{8}{/tex}

{tex}A = 173.25{/tex} {tex}\text{cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: Two non-overlapping wiping blades, each of length 35 cm, sweep through an angle of 100°. What is the total area cleaned by both blades in one sweep? (Use {tex}\pi = 22/7{/tex})
questions.explanation:
Given:

Length of each blade (radius) {tex}r = 35{/tex} cm

Angle swept by each blade {tex}\theta = 100^\circ{/tex}

Number of blades = 2

{tex}\pi = 22/7{/tex}

First, calculate the area cleaned by one blade using the sector area formula:

{tex}A_{one\_blade} = \frac{\theta}{360^\circ} \times \pi r^2{/tex}

{tex}A_{one\_blade} = \frac{100}{360} \times \frac{22}{7} \times (35)^2{/tex}
